text:
FC METALL NORD — GAME MODEL

Our identity: verticality over possession. Press from the front. Win the ball in the opponent's half. Quick transitions. Flank overload with compact rest-defence.

FORMATION: 4-3-3

IN POSSESSION:
- Verticality: forward first, sideways only as a last resort
- The 6 is the metronome — always available, always forward-thinking
- The 10 drifts wide to create the flank overload — winger pins, 10 drifts, fullback underlaps
- The 9 peels into the channel — drags a marker, opens the pocket for the 10
- Build patience against a mid-block — circulate, wait for the trigger, then vertical
- Switch isolation: if the weak side is 1v1, trust your man. No double-team.
- Transition lock: when we win it high, two touches max. Shot or cross. No recycling.

OUT OF POSSESSION:
- Press from the front — the 9 is the first defender
- First touch pressure: the moment their GK has it, the 9 cuts the passing lane to the CB
- Pressing trigger: ball-side winger collapses on the CB when he receives facing his own goal — green light
- Compact block: when we lose it, everyone within 30m of the ball. No one jogs back — sprint to position.
- Rest-defence: back four plus the 6, compact, 18-20m between lines. No chasing shadows.
- The 6 marks their 6. The 8 presses their 8. The 10 covers the pocket. Everyone has a role.
- Discipline over chasing. We press with structure, not with everyone.

TRANSITION:
- When we win it in their half: transition lock. Two touches, shot or cross. No recycling.
- When we win it in our half: verticality. First look forward to the 10 or into the channel for the 9.
- The front three sprint forward the moment we win it. The 6 and 8 support. The back four holds.

SET PIECES:
- Kross attacks the ball. Hartmann near post run. Vogt stays for rest-defence.
- Defending: man-marking, not zonal. Kross on their biggest threat. Vogt sweeps. Brandt commands.
- Don't concede cheap fouls in our half.

GK:
- Sweeper-keeper. Good with feet. Commands the high line.
- Distribution: 70% short to CBs, 30% long to channels.
- High line is non-negotiable. Brandt makes that possible.

TERMINOLOGY:
- Pressing trigger: ball-side winger collapses on the CB facing his own goal
- Rest-defence: back four plus 6, compact, 18-20m between lines
- Verticality: forward first, sideways only as a last resort
- Flank overload: winger pins, 10 drifts, fullback underlaps
- Compact block: everyone within 30m of the ball when we lose it
- First touch pressure: 9 cuts the lane to the CB when GK has it
- Channel run: 9 peels into the channel between CB and FB
- Switch isolation: weak side 1v1, trust your man
- Build patience: circulate against a mid-block, wait for the trigger
- Transition lock: win it high, two touches, shot or cross, no recycling

PRINCIPLES (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
1. Press from the front — the 9 is the first defender
2. Verticality: forward first, sideways only as a last resort
3. Compact rest-defence: never more than 20m between lines
4. Flank overload: create 2v1s on the ball side
5. Transition speed: win it, two touches, shot or cross
6. Trust the 1v1 on the weak side — don't overcommit
7. The 6 is the metronome — always available, always forward-thinking
8. GK plays as a sweeper-keeper, high line is non-negotiable

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs SV HAFEN UNITED (Rückrunde)

RESULT: 3-1 W

FIRST HALF:
- Pressed Mahler from the start. Hartmann cut the lane to Fass — Mahler went long twice in the first 5 minutes. Riedel won the second ball both times. Good.
- Channel run pattern worked: Hartmann peeled into the channel, Fass couldn't recover. Wendt found him twice. First chance at 8 min — Hartmann shot wide. Second chance at 22 min — GOAL. Hartmann left foot, near post. Exactly the pattern.
- Flank overload on the right: Krüger pinned their LB, Wendt drifted wide, Heil underlaps. Created three 2v1s in the first half. Their LB was struggling.
- Compact block was good. 18-20m between lines. No counters conceded. Hafen had possession but couldn't penetrate.
- Second goal at 35 min: transition lock. Schäfer won it in their half, two touches to Hartmann, cross, Krüger finished. Perfect transition. Two touches, no recycling.
- Hafen pulled one back at 43 min: set piece. Kross lost Pfeiffer's run. Need to fix the marking. But overall, good first half.

SECOND HALF:
- Hafen changed to 3-5-2 — tried to match up. Didn't work. Their wingbacks pushed high, channels opened.
- Third goal at 67 min: channel run again. Hartmann peeled, Wendt found him, cutback to Schäfer arriving late. Box-to-box, late run. That's his pattern.
- Schäfer tired at 72 min — subbed him. Fresh 8 came on. Good timing.
- Heil got caught high once at 78 min — their counter. Auer covered. Compact block held. Need to remind Heil: no overlapping when the counter is on.
- Last 10 minutes: compact block, saw it out. Professional.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- Channel run is our most dangerous pattern. Fass can't recover. Train it more.
- Transition lock works when we execute — two touches, no recycling.
- Set piece defending needs work. Kross lost his man.
- Schäfer's late runs are a weapon. But sub him at 65, not 72.

MAN OF THE MATCH: Hartmann. Two goals, pressing, channel runs. The 9 doing everything.

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs FC SÜDWEST STERN (Hinrunde)

RESULT: 0-1 L

FIRST HALF:
- We had 70% possession. Passed the ball sideways. Couldn't penetrate. That's not verticality — that's recycling.
- Stern sat in a deep 4-4-2. Absorbed pressure. We didn't have the patience to break them down. Forced shots from distance — Engel saved everything. He's a great shot-stopper.
- No channel runs. Hartmann was isolated — we didn't get the ball into the channel. Recycling, not vertical.
- Flank overload didn't work — Wendt stayed central, didn't drift wide. No 2v1s created.
- The goal: 38 min. Lost transition in their half. Heil was caught high — overlapping when he shouldn't have. Köhler broke on the right. Auer was isolated 1v1. Compact block was nowhere. 18-20m between lines? More like 40m. That's a rest-defence failure.

SECOND HALF:
- Same pattern. 70% possession, no goals. We kept recycling. No verticality. No channel runs. No overloads.
- Schäfer tired at 65 — should have subbed earlier. The press dropped.
- Loth came on at 60 — stayed wide but never got the switch. We never switched the point of attack.
- Hartmann had one chance at 75 — channel run, but the ball was late. Fass recovered. If the ball is earlier, that's a goal.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- We didn't play our game. Recycling is not verticality. Sideways is not forward.
- Heil got caught high — against a counter team, no overlapping. Compact block. Auer 1v1 only.
- No flank overloads. Wendt didn't drift. No 2v1s. Predictable.
- No channel runs. We didn't feed Hartmann. The 9 can't score if we don't find the channel.
- Set pieces: we had 8 corners, scored from none. Kross won headers but no one was on the second ball.

WHAT TO FIX:
1. Verticality. Stop recycling. Forward first.
2. Channel runs. Find Hartmann. Early balls, not late.
3. Flank overload. Wendt drifts wide. 2v1s.
4. Rest-defence. Compact block. 18-20m. No overlapping against counters.
5. Build patience against a low block. Circulate with purpose, not just to keep the ball.

This was not FC Metall Nord. We need to be better.

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs TSV BERGLAND (Hinrunde)

RESULT: 0-0 D

FIRST HALF:
- Bergland sat in a 3-5-2 mid-block. Compact, passive out of possession. We couldn't break them down.
- Forced it. No build patience. Tried to play through the middle — their three CBs blocked the channels. No verticality — we went sideways.
- Channel runs: Hartmann tried but their middle CB Stahl stepped and covered. We didn't exploit the pocket behind Stahl. Wendt was marked tight.
- Flank overload: their WBR Dorn got forward but we didn't punish him on the counter. Krüger pinned him but the 10 didn't drift. No 2v1s.
- Set pieces: they're a threat. Pfeiffer won headers against Kross — that's a physical mismatch. Need to match him better.
- Long balls: they played direct to twin strikers. Kross and Vogt dealt with it — compact block was good. 18-20m between lines. No goals conceded from open play.

SECOND HALF:
- More of the same. We had the ball, couldn't score. Bergland was comfortable in their block.
- Schäfer pressed Stahl when he stepped — that worked. Stahl turned it over once. But we didn't capitalize.
- Loth came on at 55 — stayed wide, stretched their back 3. Better. But we didn't switch to him enough.
- Hartmann had a chance at 68 — channel run, but their FB recovered. 3-at-the-back covers the channel better than 4-at-the-back.
- Last 20: we pushed, they countered. Kross and Vogt had to deal with three counter situations. Compact block held.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- Build patience. Against a mid-block, don't force it. Circulate, wait for the trigger.
- Stahl steps — exploit the pocket behind him. Wendt needs to find that space.
- Flank overload: Krüger pins Dorn, 10 underlaps. Dorn doesn't track back — punish him.
- Set pieces: Kross needs help with Pfeiffer. Vogt can take him — Kross sweeps.
- Against 3-at-the-back: channel runs are harder. Switch the point of attack. Loth isolation on the weak side.

WHAT TO FIX:
1. Build patience against a mid-block.
2. Exploit the pocket when their CB steps.
3. Switch the point of attack — don't just play one side.
4. Set piece marking on big strikers.

TRAINING SESSION — PRESSING DRILLS

WARM-UP (15 min):
- Rondos 4v2 in tight spaces. Focus on reaction speed after losing the ball.
- Transition to pressing shape immediately when the ball is lost.

DRILL 1: FIRST TOUCH PRESSURE (20 min)
- Setup: GK + 2 CBs vs 9 + 2 wingers
- GK plays to CB. The 9 cuts the passing lane to the near CB. Ball-side winger collapses on the CB when he receives facing his own goal.
- Key: read the CB's body shape. If he's facing his own goal — green light. If he's facing forward — hold.
- Hartmann: good at reading the body shape. Krüger: needs to be faster on the trigger.
- Reps: 20 each side.

DRILL 2: COMPACT BLOCK (15 min)
- Setup: 11v11 half-pitch. Defending team in compact block.
- Coach calls "lose it" — everyone sprints to position. 18-20m between lines. Everyone within 30m of the ball.
- Key: no jogging. Sprint to position. The block forms in 3 seconds.
- Heil: caught jogging twice. Reminder: no one jogs back.
- Reps: 15.

DRILL 3: TRANSITION LOCK (25 min)
- Setup: 7v7 in the final third. Coach plays a ball to the defending team.
- When they win it: two touches max. Shot or cross. No recycling.
- Key: first touch control, second touch finish. If the shot isn't on, cross. If the cross isn't on, find the 9 in the channel.
- Schäfer: excellent. Two touches every time. Hartmann: tried to dribble once — stopped him. Two touches.
- Reps: 25.

COOL-DOWN (15 min):
- Light jog, stretching. Review the principles. Press from the front. Compact block. Transition lock.

NOTES:
- Schäfer is fit but tires after 70. Monitor his load.
- Heil needs to work on when to overlap and when to hold. He's getting caught high.
- Riedel is sharp — metronome. No errors today.
- Wendt needs to drift more in the overload drill. He's staying central.

TRAINING SESSION — FLANK OVERLOAD PATTERNS

WARM-UP (15 min):
- Passing patterns: CB → 6 → 10 → RW → cross. Quick, one-touch where possible.
- Focus on timing of movements.

DRILL 1: 2v1 OVERLOAD (25 min)
- Setup: RW + 10 vs LB in a 20m channel. RB underlaps as a third option.
- Krüger pins the LB. Wendt drifts wide to create the 2v1. Heil underlaps.
- Key: Wendt's drift is the trigger. If he stays central, it's 1v1. If he drifts, it's 2v1.
- Progression: add a CB to make it 3v2. The 9 peels into the channel as a fourth option.
- Krüger: good at pinning. Wendt: drifting better today. Heil: underlap timing improved.
- Reps: 20 right side, 15 left side.

DRILL 2: SWITCH ISOLATION (20 min)
- Setup: full-pitch. Ball starts on the right (overload). Coach calls "switch" — ball goes to the left.
- Loth is isolated 1v1 against the RB. Trust your man. No double-team.
- Key: the switch must be fast. If it's slow, the defense shifts and the 1v1 becomes 2v2.
- Loth: good 1v1. Beat his man three times. But tracks back late — remind him.
- Reps: 15.

DRILL 3: CHANNEL RUN + POCKET (20 min)
- Setup: 9 + 10 vs 2 CBs in the half-space.
- Hartmann peels into the channel between CB and FB. Wendt finds him from the pocket.
- Key: the ball must be early. If it's late, the CB recovers.
- Hartmann: channel runs are sharp. Wendt: finding him earlier today. Good connection.
- Progression: add the 6 as the passer. Riedel receives between lines, first look forward to Wendt or into the channel for Hartmann.
- Riedel: verticality. First look forward. Good.
- Reps: 20.

COOL-DOWN (10 min):
- Review. Flank overload: winger pins, 10 drifts, fullback underlaps. Channel run: 9 peels, 10 finds. Switch isolation: trust the 1v1.

NOTES:
- Wendt and Hartmann connection is key. They need to play together more.
- Heil's underlap timing is better but still needs work.
- Loth's 1v1 is a weapon. But his tracking back is a liability.
- Riedel's verticality is consistent. First look forward every time.

PLAYER PROFILES — FC METALL NORD

GK — Lukas Brandt
Sweeper-keeper, good with feet, commands the high line. Distribution: 70% short to CBs, 30% long to channels. High line is non-negotiable — Brandt makes that possible. Composed under pressure, opposite of a hesitant GK. If pressed, plays short to Kross or Vogt — beats the press.

RB — Marco Heil
Attacking fullback, underlaps well, recovery pace. Tends to get caught high on counters — that's the weakness. Against counter-threat teams: no overlapping, underlap only, stay compact. Against passive teams: can overlap, 6 covers. Needs to read the game better — when to go, when to hold.

CB — Stefan Kross
Left-footed, aggressive in the air, leader. Steps into midfield when their 6 drops. Organizes the marking on set pieces. Physical — matches big strikers. Weakness: can lose his man on set piece runs. Communication is his strength — calls who challenges, who covers, who sweeps.

CB — Daniel Vogt
Right-footed, composed, reads the game. Stays as cover when Kross steps. Sweeps on set pieces. Fast enough to cover channels. The calm head — if Kross is the leader, Vogt is the anchor. Doesn't make mistakes. Reliable.

LB — Philipp Auer
Defensive-minded, stays compact, rarely overlaps. Good 1v1 defender. Against counter-threat teams: stays compact, no overlapping. 1v1 only. The defensive fullback — balances Heil's attacking tendencies. Doesn't get caught high. Reliable in the compact block.

CM (6) — Jonas Riedel
Metronome, always available, forward-thinking. Shield for the back four. First look is forward — to the 10 or into the channel for the 9. Don't go sideways. Weakness: can be pressed into errors. If pressed from behind, play back to Brandt, reset. The 6 is the metronome — always available, always in position.

CM (8) — Erik Schäfer
Box-to-box, late runs into the box, pressing engine. The legs of the team. When the flank overload draws the defense right, Schäfer arrives late on the left edge of the box. Presses their 6 when he drops. Weakness: tires after 70 minutes. Sub at 65, not 72. Fresh 8 with 20 minutes to go.

CM (10) — Niklas Wendt
Creative, drifts wide to create overloads, vision. Not a defender — needs cover. The 10's drift is what creates the flank overload. If he stays central, it's 1v1. If he drifts, it's 2v1. Finds the pocket when their CB steps. Link-up with Hartmann is key — the channel run pattern depends on this connection.

RW — Tomas Krüger
Pacey, pins the FB, cuts inside to shoot. Right foot dominant. Pressing trigger player — reads the CB's body shape and goes. Pins the FB so the 10 can drift and create the 2v1. Against teams with high fullbacks: pins, then the channel opens for Hartmann.

ST (9) — Felix Hartmann
Channel runner, holds up play, presses the CB. Left foot. First defender — cuts the lane to the CB when the GK has it. Peels into the channel — drags a marker, opens the pocket for the 10. Link-up with the 10 is key. If we don't feed him, he drifts out of the game. Verticality: find the channel, find Hartmann.

LW — Andreas Loth
Isolation specialist, 1v1 threat, left foot. Stays wide. When we switch the point of attack, Loth takes his man 1v1. Trust your man — no double-team. Weakness: tracks back late. Against counter-threat teams: if he doesn't track back, sub him. But he starts — his 1v1 is a weapon.

OPPONENT ANALYSIS — SV HAFEN UNITED

FORMATION: 4-2-3-1
STYLE: Possession-based. Patient build-up, switch play. Weak to high press.

KEY PLAYERS:
- Sven Mahler (GK): Hesitant under pressure. Tends to play long when pressed. That's where we win the second ball. First touch pressure from Hartmann — cut the lane to Fass, force the long ball.
- Robert Fass (CB): Slow to turn. If Hartmann peels into the channel, Fass can't recover. That's our channel run pattern — exploit it.
- Kai Bohn (CM 6): Good shield but doesn't like being pressed from behind. Riedel marks him when we have the ball. If Bohn drops to receive, Schäfer presses from behind.

WEAKNESSES:
1. GK uncomfortable under pressure — first touch pressure, force long ball, win second ball.
2. CBs slow to turn — channel runs. Fass can't recover.
3. Fullbacks push high — channels open. Transition lock when we win it.
4. No plan B vs low block — if we sit compact, they'll force passes into our pressing trap.

STRENGTHS:
1. Midfield control — their 6 shields well.
2. Set pieces — organized. Kross needs to mark their biggest threat.
3. Experienced — they don't panic under pressure (except Mahler).

LAST MATCH: 2-1 loss to FC Metall Nord (Hinrunde). Conceded from a channel run by Hartmann after pressing their CB into a mistake. Exactly our pattern.

GAME PLAN:
- First touch pressure on Mahler. Hartmann cuts the lane to Fass.
- Channel runs: Hartmann peels, Fass can't recover. Wendt finds him.
- Flank overload on the right: Krüger pins their LB, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps.
- Rest-defence: compact, 18-20m. Heil doesn't overlap if their counter is on.
- Set pieces: Kross on their biggest threat. Don't concede cheap fouls.

OPPONENT ANALYSIS — TSV BERGLAND

FORMATION: 3-5-2
STYLE: Direct. Long balls to twin strikers, wingbacks provide width. Compact mid-block.

KEY PLAYERS:
- Uwe Stahl (CB middle): Aggressive stepper. If he steps, Wendt finds the pocket behind him. Schäfer presses when he steps.
- Mike Dorn (WBR): Gets forward but doesn't track back fast. Krüger pins him, 10 underlaps. 2v1 available.
- Lars Pfeiffer (ST): Big, physical, wins headers. Kross needs to match him physically. If Kross struggles, Vogt takes him.

WEAKNESSES:
1. Wingbacks caught high — 2v1s available. Dorn doesn't track back.
2. 3-at-the-back vulnerable to channel runs — but harder than 4-at-the-back. Switch the point of attack.
3. No pressing structure — passive out of possession. We have time on the ball.
4. Mid-block can be broken with patience — circulate, wait for the trigger.

STRENGTHS:
1. Physical twin strikers — Pfeiffer wins headers. Set piece threat.
2. Counter-attack from deep — long balls to the strikers.
3. Compact block — hard to break down. We drew 0-0 last time.

LAST MATCH: 0-0 draw. We struggled against their mid-block — couldn't break them down. Need more patience in build-up.

GAME PLAN:
- Build patience. Don't force it. Circulate, wait for Stahl to step, then play through.
- Flank overload: Krüger pins Dorn, Wendt underlaps. Dorn doesn't track back — punish him.
- Channel runs: harder against 3-at-the-back. Switch to Loth on the weak side — isolation.
- Set pieces: Kross on Pfeiffer. If struggling, Vogt takes him, Kross sweeps.
- Rest-defence: compact, 18-20m. They counter from deep — no one caught high.

OPPONENT ANALYSIS — FC SÜDWEST STERN

FORMATION: 4-4-2
STYLE: Counter-attacking. Deep block, absorb pressure, counter with two fast wingers.

KEY PLAYERS:
- Tom Engel (GK): Great shot-stopper, weak distribution. Press him and he'll go long — second ball is ours. Don't shoot from distance — he saves everything. Work it into the box.
- Jan Köhler (RW): Rapid counter threat. Auer must stay compact, no overlapping. 1v1 only. If Auer overlaps, Köhler has space to counter.
- Max Ritter (ST): Poacher, stays central. Kross marks him tight, Vogt sweeps. Don't let him get a touch in the box.

WEAKNESSES:
1. Deep block can be pulled apart by switches — circulate, switch the point of attack.
2. No pressing at all — we have time on the ball. Build patience.
3. Vulnerable to overloads on both flanks — Krüger pins, Wendt drifts on the right. Switch to Loth on the left.

STRENGTHS:
1. Two rapid wingers for counters — Köhler on the right, their LW on the left.
2. Disciplined block — hard to break down. We had 70% possession and lost.
3. GK is excellent shot-stopper — don't shoot from distance.

LAST MATCH: 1-0 win for Stern. We had 70% possession, couldn't score. They countered from a lost transition — Heil was caught high. That's a rest-defence failure.

GAME PLAN:
- Build patience. Don't force it. Circulate, switch the point of attack, pull their block apart.
- No shooting from distance — Engel saves everything. Work it into the box.
- Flank overloads on both sides: right (Krüger pins, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps), left (Loth isolation).
- Rest-defence: COMPACT. Heil does not overlap. Auer 1v1 only. 18-20m between lines.
- Transition lock: when we win it high, two touches, shot or cross. No recycling.
- Don't concede counters. If we lose it, compact block immediately. Everyone within 30m.

TRAINING SESSION — TRANSITION & COUNTER

WARM-UP (15 min):
- 5v2 rondos in tight grids. Focus on reaction speed when ball is lost.
- Sprint to pressing shape immediately upon turnover.

DRILL 1: TRANSITION LOCK (30 min)
- Setup: 7v7 in final third. Coach plays ball to defending team.
- When they win it: TWO TOUCHES MAX. Shot or cross. No recycling.
- Hartmann: tried to dribble once. Stopped him. Two touches. That's the rule.
- Schäfer: excellent. Two touches every time. Late run into the box — that's his pattern.
- Krüger: cross on the second touch. Good delivery.
- Reps: 30. High intensity.

DRILL 2: COUNTER-PRESS (20 min)
- Setup: 11v11 half pitch. Attacking team loses the ball on a transition.
- The moment we lose it: compact block. Everyone within 30m. Sprint to position.
- Then: counter-press. The nearest 3 players press immediately. The rest form the block.
- If we win it back: transition lock. Two touches, shot or cross.
- Heil: got caught high twice. Reminder: against counter teams, no overlapping.
- Reps: 20.

DRILL 3: CHANNEL RUN FINISHING (15 min)
- Setup: 9 + 10 vs 1 CB in the half-space.
- Wendt finds Hartmann in the channel. Hartmann receives, first touch forward, second touch finish.
- Left foot, near post. That's his pattern.
- Progression: add a FB. Hartmann peels between CB and FB. Realistic.
- Hartmann: 8/10 finishes. Good. The two he missed — he tried to place it too far. Trust the near post.
- Reps: 15.

COOL-DOWN (10 min):
- Review. Transition lock: two touches, shot or cross. Counter-press: compact block first, then press. Channel run: near post, trust it.

NOTES:
- Schäfer is sharp but monitor his load. Three drills at high intensity — he'll be sore tomorrow.
- Heil needs the counter-press drill more than anyone. He's the one who gets caught high.
- Hartmann's finishing is reliable. The channel run → finish is our most dangerous pattern.
- Wendt's passing from the pocket is improving. The connection with Hartmann is getting automatic.

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs TSV BERGLAND (Rückrunde)

RESULT: 2-0 W

FIRST HALF:
- Build patience. That was the difference from the Hinrunde. We didn't force it. Circulated at the back, waited for Stahl to step.
- Stahl stepped at 12 min — Wendt found the pocket behind him. Played to Hartmann in the channel. Hartmann finished near post. 1-0. Exactly the pattern we trained.
- Flank overload on the right: Krüger pinned Dorn, Wendt drifted, Heil underlaps. Dorn doesn't track back — we exploited it all game. Created five 2v1s in the first half.
- Compact block was excellent. 18-20m between lines. Bergland played long to Pfeiffer — Kross matched him physically, Vogt swept. No chances conceded.
- Set pieces: Kross on Pfeiffer. Body-to-body. Pfeiffer won one header but Vogt cleared the second ball. Better than Hinrunde.

SECOND HALF:
- Bergland pushed their wingbacks higher — tried to stretch us. Channels opened. We countered twice.
- Second goal at 58 min: transition lock. Schäfer won it in their half, two touches to Krüger, cross, Hartmann headed it in. Two touches. No recycling. Perfect.
- Subbed Schäfer at 63 — fresh 8. Good timing. Schäfer was starting to tire.
- Loth came on at 70 — stayed wide, stretched their back 3. Got the switch three times. Created one chance from isolation. Good.
- Last 20: compact block, saw it out. Professional. No panic.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- Build patience works against a mid-block. Don't force it. Wait for the trigger.
- Stahl steps — exploit the pocket. That's the pattern against 3-at-the-back.
- Dorn doesn't track back — flank overload all day. Krüger pins, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps.
- Kross on Pfeiffer: physical match worked. Vogt sweeps. Set piece defending fixed.
- Schäfer sub at 63 — perfect timing. Fresh 8 maintained the press.

MAN OF THE MATCH: Wendt. Two assists, found the pocket every time Stahl stepped. The 10 doing exactly what we need.

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs FC SÜDWEST STERN (Rückrunde)

RESULT: 2-1 W

FIRST HALF:
- Build patience. Last time we had 70% possession and lost. This time: circulate with purpose, not just to keep the ball.
- Switched the point of attack four times in the first 15 minutes. Pulled their deep block apart. Loth was isolated 1v1 on the left — beat his man twice. Trust the 1v1.
- First goal at 22 min: flank overload on the right. Krüger pinned, Wendt drifted, Heil underlaps. Wendt crossed, Hartmann near post. 1-0. The pattern works against any block.
- Rest-defence: COMPACT. Heil did not overlap. Auer 1v1 only. 18-20m between lines. Köhler had no space to counter.
- Engel saved two shots from distance — don't shoot from distance. Work it into the box. That's the lesson from last time.

SECOND HALF:
- Stern equalized at 55 min: set piece. Kross lost his man on the near post. We need to fix set piece marking — it's been a pattern.
- Response: verticality. Didn't panic. Riedel received between lines, first look forward to Wendt. Wendt found Hartmann in the channel. Hartmann cutback to Schäfer arriving late. 2-1. Box-to-box, late run. That's Schäfer's pattern.
- Subbed Schäfer at 67 — he was gassed. Fresh 8 came on. Press maintained.
- Heil: no overlapping. Stayed compact. Köhler had one chance — Auer 1v1, won it. Compact block held.
- Last 10: compact block, saw it out. No counter conceded. That's the difference from Hinrunde.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
- Build patience + switch the point of attack = breaks a deep block.
- No shooting from distance against a good shot-stopper. Work it into the box.
- Rest-defence: compact. No overlapping against counter teams. That's the fix from last time.
- Set piece defending: still a weakness. Kross lost his man. Need to train this more.
- Schäfer's late runs are a weapon. But sub at 65, not 67.

MAN OF THE MATCH: Riedel. Metronome. Always available, always forward-thinking. Two assists from the 6 position. The metronome doing his job.

SEASON REVIEW — FC METALL NORD

RECORD: 14W 5D 3L. 47 goals scored, 18 conceded.

WHAT WORKED:
- Press from the front. The 9 as first defender — Hartmann pressed every GK, every CB. That's our identity and it worked. 12 of our 47 goals came from transition lock — winning it high, two touches, shot or cross.
- Channel runs. Hartmann in the channel, Wendt finding him from the pocket. Our most dangerous pattern. 15 goals from this pattern alone.
- Flank overloads. Krüger pins, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps. Created 2v1s all season. The right side was our weapon.
- Compact block. 18-20m between lines. Only 18 goals conceded — best defensive record in the league. Kross and Vogt were excellent.
- Riedel as metronome. Always available, always forward-thinking. The 6 is the foundation of everything we do.

WHAT DIDN'T WORK:
- Set piece defending. 6 of 18 goals conceded from set pieces. Kross loses his man on the near post. Need to train this more in the off-season.
- Schäfer's fitness. Tires after 70. We subbed him late sometimes — 72, 75. Should be 65. Need a backup 8.
- Loth's tracking back. Against counter teams, he was a liability. Subbed him three times for not tracking back. Need a defensive winger option.
- Breaking down mid-blocks. Against Bergland in the Hinrunde, we drew 0-0. We forced it. Build patience was the fix — and it worked in the Rückrunde (2-0 W).

GOALS FOR NEXT SEASON:
1. Fix set piece defending. Kross + Vogt organization. Train it every week.
2. Sign a backup 8. Schäfer can't play 90 every game.
3. Sign a defensive winger. Loth needs competition.
4. More channel run patterns. Add variations — cutback to the 8, layoff to the 10.
5. Better transition from the back. Brandt's distribution: more channel balls, less recycling.

IDENTITY: We are FC Metall Nord. Verticality over possession. Press from the front. Compact rest-defence. Flank overloads. Transition lock. The game model works. The execution gets better. Same identity, higher level.

TRAINING SESSION — SET PIECES

WARM-UP (15 min):
- Crossing and finishing drill. Wingers deliver from both flanks, strikers attack near and far post.
- Focus on timing of runs — arrive at the same time as the ball, not early.

DRILL 1: CORNER ROUTINES (25 min)
- Routine 1 (standard): Kruger delivers in-swinging from the right. Kross attacks near post. Hartmann makes a near-post run to draw a defender. Vogt stays for rest-defence with Riedel.
- Routine 2 (short): Kruger plays short to Wendt. Wendt dribbles toward the box. Defense shifts. Kross peels to back post. Wendt crosses to Kross.
- Routine 3 (channel): Hartmann peels into the channel at the near post. Drags two defenders. Delivery goes to back post where Schaefer arrives late.
- Kross: won 8 of 12 headers. Good. The delivery needs to be faster — Kruger, whip it in, don't float it.
- Reps: 10 per routine.

DRILL 2: FREE KICK DEFENDING (20 min)
- Setup: 11v11 in the defensive third. Coach delivers free kicks from various positions.
- Man-marking: Kross on the biggest target. Vogt sweeps. Brandt commands. Everyone has a man.
- Key: don't lose your man. Block the run-up. Body position. If the ball is cleared, sprint out — don't let them recycle.
- Kross: lost his man once on the near post. That's the pattern we need to fix. Body-to-body, no gaps.
- Reps: 15.

DRILL 3: THROW-IN ROUTINES (10 min)
- Long throw from Auer on the left. Kross attacks the near post. Hartmann peels to the back.
- Short throw: quick restart. Riedel receives, plays forward immediately. Verticality even from throw-ins.
- Reps: 10.

COOL-DOWN (10 min):
- Review. Three corner routines. Man-marking on free kicks. Sprint out after clearance. Set pieces are part of the game model — verticality (delivery into the box), compact (marking), transition (sprint out).

NOTES:
- Kross's heading is our biggest set piece weapon. Delivery needs to be fast, not floated.
- Hartmann's near-post run draws defenders — creates space for Kross and Schaefer.
- Set piece defending: Kross lost his man once. That's one too many. Body-to-body.

MATCH NOTES — FC METALL NORD vs SV HAFEN UNITED (Rueckrunde, detailed)

RESULT: 3-1 W. GOALS: Hartmann 22', Krueger 35', Schaefer 67'. Conceded: 43' set piece.

TACTICAL ANALYSIS:

FIRST HALF:
- Pressing: Hartmann cut the lane to Fass from the first whistle. Mahler went long twice in 5 minutes. Riedel won both second balls. Pressing trigger fired 12 times in the first half — excellent.
- Channel runs: Hartmann peeled into the channel 8 times. Fass couldn't recover 6 times. Goal at 22' — channel run, Wendt found him, left foot near post. Exactly the pattern.
- Flank overload: Krueger pinned their LB, Wendt drifted wide, Heil underlaps. Created 5 2v1s in the first half. Their LB was struggling — booked at 30'.
- Second goal at 35': transition lock. Schaefer won it in their half, two touches to Hartmann, cross, Krueger finished. Two touches. No recycling. Perfect transition.
- Compact block: 18-20m between lines. No counters conceded from open play. Hafen had 62% possession but couldn't penetrate.
- Conceded at 43': set piece. Kross lost Pfeiffer's run on the near post. Ball flicked on, their 10 finished. Set piece defending — the pattern we need to fix.

SECOND HALF:
- Hafen changed to 3-5-2 — tried to match up. Didn't work. Their wingbacks pushed high, channels opened.
- Third goal at 67': channel run again. Hartmann peeled, Wendt found him, cutback to Schaefer arriving late. Box-to-box, late run. Schaefer's pattern.
- Schaefer subbed at 72' — he was gassed. Fresh 8 came on. Good timing.
- Heil caught high once at 78' — their counter. Auer covered. Compact block held. Reminder: no overlapping when the counter is on.
- Last 10: compact block, saw it out. Professional.

PLAYER RATINGS (1-5, game model criteria):
- Hartmann: 4.8 — two goals, pressing relentless, channel runs lethal. MOTM.
- Wendt: 4.6 — two assists, found the pocket every time. Drift created overloads.
- Schaefer: 4.5 — one goal, transition lock executed, late runs dangerous. Tired at 70.
- Riedel: 4.4 — metronome, won second balls, forward-thinking. One error under pressure.
- Krueger: 4.3 — one goal, pinned their LB, pressing trigger fired 8 times.
- Kross: 3.8 — good in the air, organized the line. But lost his man on the set piece. That's the fix.
- Vogt: 4.2 — swept well, covered when Kross stepped. Reliable.
- Heil: 3.9 — good underlaps, but caught high once. Rest-defence reminder needed.
- Auer: 4.0 — compact, 1v1 defending solid. No overlapping. Did his job.
- Brandt: 4.1 — composed, distribution 75% short. One good save.
- Loth: 3.7 — came on at 70, stayed wide. Got the switch twice. Tracks back late.

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
1. Channel run is our most dangerous pattern. Fass can't recover. Train it more.
2. Transition lock works when we execute — two touches, no recycling.
3. Set piece defending needs work. Kross lost his man. Train body-to-body marking.
4. Schaefer's late runs are a weapon. Sub at 65, not 72.
5. Pressing trigger fired 19 times total. Excellent intensity for 90 minutes.

OPPONENT ANALYSIS — SC Eichenwald 09

FORMATION: 4-2-3-1 (attacking) / 4-4-2 (defending)
STYLE: Flexible. Attacks with 4-2-3-1, defends in 4-4-2. The 10 drops into midfield to make a double pivot. Patient build-up, switches play, dangerous on the counter.

KEY PLAYERS:
- Tim Bauer (GK): Good with feet, comfortable under pressure. Unlike Mahler, he won't crack under first touch pressure. Need a different approach — press the CBs, not the GK.
- Jonas Wolf (CB): Right-footed, aggressive, likes to step into midfield. If he steps, Wendt finds the pocket behind him. Similar to Bergland's Stahl.
- Max Horn (CM 10): Their best player. Drops deep to receive, turns, plays forward. If we let him turn, he's dangerous. Riedel must mark him tight. Don't let him face forward.
- Lukas Berg (RW): Fast, direct, cuts inside onto his left foot. Auer must show him wide — don't let him come inside. 1v1 only. No overlapping.

WEAKNESSES:
1. CB Wolf steps aggressively — if he steps, the pocket opens for Wendt. Schaefer presses when he steps.
2. Their LB is slow — Krueger pins him, Wendt drifts. 2v1 on the right.
3. Counter-attacking — they commit players forward. If we win it, transition lock. Channels open.
4. Set piece defending — they've conceded 4 from corners this season. Kross attacks the ball.

STRENGTHS:
1. GK Bauer is press-resistant. Don't waste energy pressing him. Press the CBs instead.
2. Horn at 10 is their engine. If he's free, they control the game. Mark him tight.
3. Flexible shape — 4-2-3-1 to 4-4-2. Hard to press if they keep switching. Read the shape.
4. Counter-attack — Berg on the right is rapid. Auer stays compact. No overlapping.

LAST MATCH: 2-2 draw with Bergland. Came back from 0-2. Flexible shape caused Bergland problems.

GAME PLAN:
- Don't press the GK. Press the CBs. Hartmann cuts the lane to Wolf, not Bauer.
- Riedel marks Horn tight. Don't let him turn. If he drops deep, Schaefer presses from behind.
- Flank overload on the right: Krueger pins their slow LB, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps.
- Channel runs: Wolf steps aggressively. When he steps, Hartmann peels into the channel. Wendt finds him.
- Rest-defence: COMPACT. Berg is their counter threat. Auer 1v1 only. No overlapping. 18-20m between lines.
- Set pieces: they've conceded from corners. Kross attacks. Three routines. Whip it in.
- Transition: they commit players forward. If we win it, channels are open. Transition lock. Two touches, shot or cross.

SEASON REVIEW — FC METALL NORD (DETAILED)

RECORD: 14W 5D 3L. 47 goals scored, 18 conceded. 1st place, Regional Liga Nord.

SEASON METRICS (game model based):
- Pressing triggers fired: 412 (avg 21/match). Target: 18+. Exceeded.
- Forward passes from 6 (Riedel): 68% forward, 32% sideways/back. Target: 65%+. Met.
- Channel runs attempted: 178. Completed: 94 (53%). Goals from channel runs: 15 (32% of total).
- Transition goals: 12 (26% of total). Two-touch finishes: 89% of transitions.
- Compact block: 18-20m maintained 84% of match time. Counters conceded: 7 (0.37/match).
- Set pieces scored: 9. Set pieces conceded: 6. Kross duel win rate: 71%.

WHAT WORKED:
1. Press from the front. Hartmann pressed every GK, every CB. 12 goals from pressing triggers. The 9 as first defender is our identity.
2. Channel runs. 15 goals from this pattern. Hartmann + Wendt connection is automatic. Fass-type CBs exposed every time.
3. Flank overloads. Krueger pins, Wendt drifts, Heil underlaps. Created 47 2v1s across the season. Right side is our weapon.
4. Compact block. Only 18 goals conceded. Best defensive record in the league. Kross and Vogt were excellent. 18-20m between lines maintained 84% of the time.
5. Riedel as metronome. 68% forward passes. Always available, always in position. The 6 is the foundation.
6. Transition lock. 12 goals from two-touch finishes. When we win it high, we score. The rule works.

WHAT DIDN'T WORK:
1. Set piece defending. 6 of 18 goals conceded from set pieces (33%). Kross loses his man on the near post. Need to train body-to-body marking every week.
2. Schaefer's fitness. Tires after 70. We subbed him late sometimes (72, 75). Should be 65. Need a backup 8.
3. Loth's tracking back. Against counter teams, he was a liability. Subbed 3 times for not tracking back. Need a defensive winger option.
4. Breaking down mid-blocks. Against Bergland in the Hinrunde, we drew 0-0. We forced it. Build patience was the fix — and it worked in the Rueckrunde (2-0 W).
5. Switch isolation underused. Loth's 1v1 is a weapon but we only switched to him 12 times all season. Need more switches.

GOALS FOR NEXT SEASON:
1. Fix set piece defending. Kross + Vogt organization. Train it every week. Target: max 3 conceded from set pieces.
2. Sign a backup 8. Schaefer can't play 90 every game. Fresh legs for the last 20.
3. Sign a defensive winger. Loth needs competition. Compact block option on the left.
4. More channel run variations. Add cutback to the 8, layoff to the 10. Don't be predictable.
5. More switches to Loth. Switch isolation is a weapon. Use it 20+ times next season.
6. Better transition from the back. Brandt's distribution: more channel balls, less recycling.

IDENTITY: We are FC Metall Nord. Verticality over possession. Press from the front. Compact rest-defence. Flank overloads. Transition lock. The game model works. The execution gets better. Same identity, higher level.

PLAYER DEVELOPMENT PLAN — FC METALL NORD

PRINCIPLES:
1. The game model is the curriculum. Every player develops within the system.
2. Principles first, position second. If you don't understand the why, the how doesn't matter.
3. The game model is non-negotiable. If you don't press, you don't play.
4. Weaknesses are revealed by the game model. Training fixes them.
5. Strengths are utilized within the game model context.

INDIVIDUAL PLANS:

Hartmann (ST 9):
- Strengths: Channel runs, pressing, link-up with Wendt. 15 goals from channel runs.
- Development: Add cutback to the 8 as a third option (currently only shoots or crosses). Improve hold-up play when service is poor.
- Training: Channel run variations. Three options — shoot, cross, cutback. Decision-making in the channel.

Wendt (CM 10):
- Strengths: Drifting wide, creating overloads, finding the pocket. 12 assists.
- Development: Defensive positioning when we lose it. Currently needs Schaefer to cover. Improve tracking back.
- Training: Transition defense. When the overload fails, sprint to compact block. 5 seconds, not 10.

Riedel (CM 6):
- Strengths: Metronome, always available, 68% forward passes.
- Development: Press resistance. Can be pressed into errors. Improve first touch under pressure.
- Training: Pressed build-up drills. Riedel receives under pressure, plays back to Brandt, resets. Composure under pressure.

Schaefer (CM 8):
- Strengths: Box-to-box, pressing engine, late runs. 8 goals from late arrivals.
- Development: Fitness management. Tires after 70. Improve stamina or accept the sub at 65.
- Training: Interval fitness. 5-minute high intensity, 3-minute recovery. Build the engine for 90.

Krueger (RW):
- Strengths: Pins the FB, pressing trigger player, cuts inside to shoot. 9 goals.
- Development: Crossing accuracy. When he pins and gets to the byline, the cross needs to be better. Too many floaters.
- Training: Crossing under pressure. Deliver fast, not floated. Whip it in.

Kross (CB):
- Strengths: Leader, aggressive in the air, steps into midfield. 71% duel win rate.
- Development: Set piece marking. Lost his man 6 times this season. Body-to-body, no gaps.
- Training: Set piece defending. Man-marking drills. Block the run-up, body position, don't lose your man.

Vogt (CB):
- Strengths: Composed, reads the game, covers when Kross steps. Reliable.
- Development: Pace. Can be exposed by fast strikers. Improve recovery speed.
- Training: Sprint intervals. Recovery runs. 20m sprints, 10 seconds rest, 15 reps.

Heil (RB):
- Strengths: Attacking fullback, underlaps well, recovery pace.
- Development: Decision-making. When to overlap, when to hold. Caught high 7 times this season.
- Training: 2v2 decision drills. When to go, when to hold. Read the counter threat.

Auer (LB):
- Strengths: Defensive-minded, compact, good 1v1 defender.
- Development: Distribution. When he wins it, the first touch is sometimes poor. Improve ball control.
- Training: First touch under pressure. Receive, control, play forward. Simple but critical.

Brandt (GK):
- Strengths: Sweeper-keeper, good with feet, commands the high line.
- Development: Distribution variety. 75% short to CBs. Add more channel balls (30% target).
- Training: Long distribution to channels. Hartmann peels, Brandt finds him. The 30% long ball.

Loth (LW):
- Strengths: 1v1 specialist, isolation threat, left foot.
- Development: Tracking back. Against counter teams, he's a liability. Improve defensive work rate.
- Training: Transition defense. When we lose it, sprint to compact block. Non-negotiable.

ACADEMY INTEGRATION:
- U17 and U19 play 4-3-3 with the same game model. Pressing trigger, channel run, compact block, transition lock.
- Terminology is taught at U15. By U17, it's muscle memory.
- Youth players promoted to first team training once a month. Learn the intensity, the speed, the physicality.
- Two U19 players training with the first team this season: MF Tom Weiss (6, metronome profile), CB Jan Roth (compact, reads the game).

KEY: The game model is the development framework. Every player has a specific plan within the system. Principles first, position second, weaknesses trained, strengths utilized. The game model makes players better because it gives them a clear standard to meet.

TACTICAL EVOLUTION PLAN — FC METALL NORD

PRINCIPLES (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
1. Press from the front — the 9 is the first defender
2. Verticality — forward first, sideways only as a last resort
3. Compact rest-defence — 18-20m between lines
4. Flank overloads — create 2v1s on the ball side
5. Transition lock — two touches, shot or cross, no recycling

EVOLUTIONS FOR NEXT SEASON (within the game model):

1. CHANNEL RUN VARIATIONS
Current: Hartmann peels, Wendt finds him, Hartmann shoots or crosses.
Evolution: Add cutback to the 8 (Schaefer arriving late) and layoff to the 10 (Wendt following up). Three options from the channel — shoot, cross, cutback. Less predictable.
Training: Channel run decision-making. Three cones — shoot, cross, cutback. Coach calls the option. Player executes.

2. SWITCH ISOLATION FREQUENCY
Current: We switch to Loth 12 times per season. Underused.
Evolution: Switch to Loth 20+ times. When the right-side overload draws their defense, switch fast. Loth 1v1 on the weak side. Trust your man.
Training: Switch drills. Right-side overload (5 reps), coach calls switch, ball goes to Loth. Loth 1v1 against the RB. Beat him or win a set piece.

3. BUILD-UP VARIATIONS
Current: Brandt to CBs, CBs to Riedel, Riedel forward. Predictable.
Evolution: Add Kross stepping into midfield with the ball. If their striker follows, Kross drives forward. Draws a marker, opens space for Wendt. Also: Brandt long to channels more often (30% target, currently 25%).
Training: Kross carrying the ball from the back. Drive forward, draw the marker, release to Wendt. Brandt long to Hartmann in the channel.

4. PRESSING TRIGGER REFINEMENT
Current: Winger collapses when CB receives facing his own goal.
Evolution: Add a second trigger — when their 6 turns and plays forward, the 8 (Schaefer) presses immediately. Two triggers, not one. More pressing opportunities.
Training: Two-trigger drills. Coach plays to CB (trigger 1 — winger collapses) or to 6 who turns (trigger 2 — 8 presses). Players read and react.

5. SET PIECE INNOVATION
Current: 3 corner routines. Kross attacks near post.
Evolution: Add a 4th routine — short corner to Wendt, who plays it back to Krueger for a cross from a different angle. The defense doesn't expect the second-phase cross.
Training: 4 routines. Practice all 4. Opponent doesn't know which one is coming.

6. REST-DEFENCE FLEXIBILITY
Current: Back four plus 6. 18-20m between lines.
Evolution: Against teams with no counter threat, push the 8 (Schaefer) into the rest-defence line, making it 6 players. More compact, more coverage. Against counter teams, 8 stays higher for the transition. Flexible rest-defence based on opponent.
Training: Rest-defence drills. Coach calls counter team or non-counter team. Players adjust the 8's position.

KEY: These are evolutions, not revolutions. The core game model stays. Press from the front. Verticality. Compact rest-defence. Flank overloads. Transition lock. The evolutions add variety within the structure. Less predictable, more effective. Same identity, higher level. That's FC Metall Nord.
